title: quercus ilex f. avellaniformis
intro: Quercus ilex f. avellaniformis, also known as the Holm Oak or Evergreen Oak, is a subspecies of Quercus ilex, a evergreen tree native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects due to the presence of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Quercus ilex f. avellaniformis include relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into various ointments and salves for topical application.
